contaminant.name: Salt and Chloride Deposits
contaminant.description: Salt-residue contamination builds up from marine deposits or road salts, where chloride ions infiltrate surfaces and crystallize into white, powdery layers that set it apart from rust's flaky reds or dirt's uniform grime. On metals like steel, it ramps up pitting and weakening, while on stones such as concrete, it triggers efflorescence and cracking—challenges that traditional scraping often worsens by scratching substrates. Laser cleaning tackles this head-on by vaporizing residues precisely, preserving material integrity without chemicals or abrasion.

fireExplosionRisk.description: No significant fire or explosion risk identified

toxicGasRisk.description: Irritant gas generation requiring respiratory protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: Half-face or full-face respirator with organic vapor/particulate cartridges, adequate ventilation

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
